title: Dry Doddington Lancaster Memorial
crew_summary: 2 of 7 killed including Sgt. Norman Isaac Langley

external_narratives.summary: 49 Squadron Association memorial page for Lancaster PB432 EA-O at Dry Doddington Church. The aircraft crashed on 26 November 1944 shortly after take-off from Fulbeck for a mission to Munich, laden with bombs and fuel. Two of seven crew killed: Sgt Langley (W/OP) and Sgt Blake (mid-upper gunner).
key_details: Crashed shortly after take-off for Munich raid
Pilot F/O Le Marquand survived with slight injuries

external_narratives.summary: Detailed PDF account titled 'The Fulbeck Tragedy' documenting the circumstances of Lancaster PB432's crash. Provides crew details and the sequence of events leading to the failed take-off.
Imperial War Museums register entries for the crew of Lancaster PB432 commemorated at Dry Doddington, including Sgt Blake and Sgt Langley.
key_details: IWM casualty register entries for Blake and Langley
